plant.description: Unique History: A crop from the high Himalayas, adopted as a sacred fasting grain (Phalahar). Known for its intensely dark flour and profound heating effect. Physical Characteristics: A fast-growing, erect, annual herb with heart-shaped leaves and clusters of tiny white or pink flowers. The fruit is a distinctive triangular achene (nut-like seed) with a dark brown/black hull. Found specifically in cold mountainous regions. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Rutin, D-chiro-inositol, Quercetin, and Fagopyrin. Fagopyrin can cause photosensitivity in extreme doses. Strict Standardizations: Standardized based on its distinctive triangular seeds and greyish-black flour. Physicochemical benchmarks: high rutin content.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Medahara (fat-relieving), Kledahara (moisture-reducing), Mutravirechaniya (diuretic), Ushna (heating), and Raktadoshakara (can vitiate blood if overused).

kuttu_atta__flour_.method: Used strictly as a base for fried flatbreads (Puris) or pancakes during winter religious fasts (Navratri). Usually mixed with potatoes/ghee to counteract its extreme dryness.

groats__kasha_.method: Toasted whole seeds cooked like rice or porridge for extreme cold weather.

tea__soba_.method: Roasted seeds infused as a circulation-boosting winter tea.

external_paste__lepa_.method: Applied sparingly to cold, stiff joints.

dosage.children: Limited use. Only in cold weather as small pancakes.
dosage.adults: Intermittent/seasonal fasting food (not a daily staple). 50-100g of flour per meal.
dosage.elderly: Use with caution in high Pitta; beneficial for cold extremities and circulation.

medicinal_uses: Expert Warming and Kapha-Drying Specialist: Kuttu is the premier winter pseudo-cereal. Its intense 'Ushna' (heating) nature helps to melt stubborn Kapha, clear cold respiratory blocks, and provide deep internal warmth in snowy mountainous regions.

Cardiovascular Support and Circulation (Rutin Source): Known scientifically for its massive Rutin (flavonoids) content, Kuttu strengthens capillary walls, prevents internal bleeding (in a paradoxical way despite being hot), and manages varicose veins and high blood pressure.

Extreme Fat-Scraping Specialist (Meda-hara): Its 'Ruksha' (dry) and highly astringent nature provided by specific fiber profiles helps in absorbing systemic 'Meda' (fat) and resolving profound water retention (Shotha).

Gluten-Free Diabetic Support: Highly beneficial for regulating blood glucose levels by slowing down carbohydrate absorption, acting as an excellent alternative for wheat-allergic and diabetic individuals.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Proteins (13-15%), Dietary Fiber, massive amounts of Flavonoids (Rutin, Quercetin), D-chiro-inositol, and Essential Minerals (Magnesium, Copper, Manganese). Rutin is the primary bioactive for its vascular protective effects.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe seasonal staple food.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: STRICTLY contraindictated in high Pitta (burning, ulcers, hyperacidity, summer season). Do not consume daily year-round as it vitiates blood (Rakta) and causes skin eruptions (Kushtha/Visarpa) due to extreme heat.
possible_side_effects: Extreme heating, skin rashes (photosensitivity from Fagopyrin), and severe constipation if not cooked with abundant ghee or fluids.
drug_interactions: None documented. D-chiro-inositol may assist Metformin.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
